nav.githubBannerSubtitle: It's open source. A star helps others find it.

chat.title: AI Chat
chat.deleteDescription: This permanently removes the chat and its messages. This can't be undone.
chat.composerPlaceholder: Ask about your spending, categories, merchants…
chat.composerHint: Enter to send, Shift + Enter for a new line
chat.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
chat.emptyTitle: What would you like to know?
chat.emptyBody: Ask anything about your transactions, categories, or trends. The agent queries your local data on demand.
chat.suggest1: How much did I spend last month?
chat.suggest2: Show my top merchants this year
chat.suggest3: Which categories went up vs the prior month?
chat.suggest4: How much did I spend on groceries in March?
chat.disabledTitle: Set up an AI provider first
chat.disabledBody: The chat assistant uses the same model as transaction categorization. Connect Claude or Ollama in Settings to start chatting.

home.trendsEmpty: Sync to see your monthly trends.
home.insightIncreaseBodyGeneric: Up versus last month. Worth a look.
home.insightSavingBody: Down versus last month. Keep it up.
home.insightOverPaceBody: About {amount} above a usual month if this pace holds.
home.insightUnderPaceBody: On pace to keep about {amount} versus a usual month.
home.topInsightsEmpty: No standout changes this month. Nice and steady.
home.noTransactionsYet: No transactions yet. Sync your bank to see activity here.
home.connectedAccountsEmpty: No accounts yet. Sync a bank to see them here.
home.flaggedEmpty: Nothing to review. You're all caught up.

activityPanel.title: What Budgeteer is running
activityPanel.footerNote: These are the only background things Budgeteer does. Ollama processes you started yourself aren't shown.

syncFailureBanner.twoFaHelper: Some banks (notably Yahav) block scraping when 2FA is on. Disable it on the bank side and try again.

aiBanner.description: Transactions won't be auto-categorized, so budgets will appear empty until you connect an AI provider.

transactions.filterScopedToList: Filters apply to the list below.
transactions.emptyNoData: No transactions yet. Click Sync Now to pull your data.
transactions.rowReviewTooltipUnsure: AI wasn't sure, worth a review
transactions.rowApproveTooltip: Keep this category and remember it for next time
transactions.eventBadgeTooltip: Grouped as a money movement between accounts, so it is not counted as spending
transactions.eventCardMatchedTooltip: This bill is covered by card {card} — individual purchases are already counted as spending
transactions.eventCardUnmatchedTooltip: Could not match this bill to a connected card — counting as spending

dashboard.nothingToCategorize: Nothing to categorize - every transaction already has a category.
dashboard.checkDevTerminal: Check the dev server terminal for full details.
dashboard.phraseOver: , over budget, time to slow down.
dashboard.phraseHeadsUp: , a touch over schedule, but easy to recover.
dashboard.setMonthlyTarget: Set a monthly target to see how you're pacing →
dashboard.emptyUncategorizedBody: You have spending this month, but none of it is assigned to a category yet. Enable an AI provider in settings and sync, or assign categories manually from the transactions table below.
dashboard.emptyNoSpendBody: When you sync transactions and they get categorized, each category will show up here with its own budget, pace, and recent activity.
dashboard.setBudgetToTrack: set a budget to track pacing

workspace.nameDescription: Shown in the sidebar switcher. Visible only to you.
workspace.dangerDescription: Permanently removes this workspace and every bank connection, transaction, category, and budget inside it. The other workspaces are untouched.
workspace.lastOneHint: You can't delete your only workspace. Create another one first.
workspace.confirmDescription: Every transaction, category, budget, and saved bank credential in this workspace will be permanently removed. This can't be undone.

general.title: General
general.description: Workspace name, monthly target, sync window, and when your monthly cycle resets.
general.monthlyTargetDescription: A single number that drives your dashboard pace verdict. Leave blank to hide the verdict.
general.noPriorHistory: No prior month history yet. Set a target you'd like to aim for.
general.syncWindowDescription: How far back each sync reaches, and the day your monthly cycle resets.
general.banksLimit: Banks limit history to roughly 12 months.
general.paydayHint: Powers daily-allowance math and the days-until-payday hint.
general.autoSyncDescription: Run sync and categorization automatically once a day, while the Budgeteer server is running.
general.runsInIsraelTime: Runs in Israel time. Disabled by default to avoid surprise bank logins.
general.timeOfDayHint: 24-hour, Asia/Jerusalem. Most banks post transactions overnight, so early morning works well.
general.atmDescription: Choose how cash withdrawals from ATMs are counted.
general.atmToggleHint: Turn this on if you log cash spending manually, so withdrawals are not counted twice. Applies to newly synced transactions.
general.balanceDescription: Your account balance today. Powers the expected month-end balance and overdraft warnings on your dashboard.
general.balanceHint: Leave empty to hide the month-end balance forecast.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Choose how Budgeteer looks. System matches your OS setting and updates automatically.
appearance.languageCardDescription: Choose the language and reading direction for Budgeteer.

bank.title: Bank accounts
bank.description: Connected institutions. Credentials are encrypted with AES-256-GCM and never leave your machine.
bank.noneConnectedPrompt: No banks connected yet. Click <addBankBold>Add bank</addBankBold> to connect one.
bank.connectPrompt: Connect this bank to sync transactions.
bank.accountLabelHint: Shown in your bank list. Use a distinct label when you connect the same bank more than once.
bank.twoFactorTokenCleared: Saved 2FA token cleared. Your next {name} sync will ask for a fresh code.
bank.disconnectFailed: Could not disconnect this bank. Please try again.
bank.twoFaNeededDescription: Use the global Sync button on the dashboard to enter the one-time code. Budgeteer will remember the token for future syncs.
bank.syncDone: Sync complete: {added, plural, one {# new} other {# new}}, {updated, plural, one {# updated} other {# updated}}, {categorized, plural, one {# categorized} other {# categorized}}
bank.accountsHint: Name each account and set how it is owned.
bank.accountSaveFailed: Couldn’t update the account. Please try again.
bank.noAccountsYet: Accounts appear here after the first sync.

ai.title: AI & automation
ai.description: How Budgeteer organizes new transactions. Switch any time; your existing categorizations stay.
ai.providerCardDescription: Switch any time. Your existing categorizations are kept.
ai.providerClaudeDesc: Fast and accurate. Paid API. Bring your own API key.
ai.providerGeminiDesc: Generous free tier from Google AI Studio. Bring your own API key.
ai.providerOllamaDesc: Free, private, runs on your machine. Needs a model download.
ai.claudeKeyCardDescription: Paste your key from console.anthropic.com. It's encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.
ai.geminiKeyCardDescription: Paste your key from aistudio.google.com/apikey. It's encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.
ai.apiKeyRequiredHint: Enter a key before saving this provider.
ai.ollamaCardDescription: Budgeteer auto-starts Ollama if it's installed but not running.
ai.geminiModelDescription: Choose a stable Gemini text model for categorization.

categories.title: Categories
categories.description: One place to set budgets, descriptions, and grouping. Click any category to edit it.
categories.deleteHint: Permanently removes this category. All assigned transactions become uncategorized.
categories.deleteParentHint: Delete each subcategory below before you can delete this group.
categories.confirmDeleteDescription: This cannot be undone. {count, plural, =0 {No transactions are assigned to this category.} one {# transaction will become uncategorized.} other {# transactions will become uncategorized.}}
categories.deletedToast: Category deleted. {count, plural, =0 {} one {# transaction uncategorized.} other {# transactions uncategorized.}}
categories.deleteHasChildren: Delete each subcategory listed for this group first.
categories.budgetedHint: Show progress vs a monthly target.
categories.parentKindMismatch: Parent must be the same kind (expense or income).
categories.parentMustBeTopLevel: Parent must be a top-level category.
categories.childHasChildren: Can't move a category that already has sub-categories under it.
categories.parentGroupHint: Use a parent group to roll spending up. Most users keep the defaults.
categories.descriptionTooLong: Description must be {max} characters or fewer.
categories.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what belongs in "{name}" and what does NOT.
categories.aiHintFooter: Sent to the AI on every categorize.

data.title: Data & privacy
data.description: Budgeteer runs locally. Your credentials are encrypted at rest and never leave your machine.
data.showBrowserHint: Opens a visible Chromium window so you can watch the scrape happen (useful for debugging or solving 2FA / captcha challenges). Also enables verbose scraper logs in your dev terminal.
data.browserVisibleSaved: Browser will be visible on next sync
data.storageCardDescription: Bank credentials and your Claude API key are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The encryption key lives at data/.encryption-key on your machine (gitignored) and is auto-generated on first run. All transaction data lives in data/budgeteer.db. To reset everything, stop the dev server and delete the data/ directory.
data.excludedDescription: Merchants whose future transactions are automatically excluded from all spending totals. Excluded transactions still appear in the list, faded.
data.dangerDescription: Wipes every synced transaction, sync run, and merchant-memory entry. Your bank credentials, AI settings, budgets, and categories stay put. Use this to start over from a clean slate. Cannot be undone.
data.confirmKeeps: Kept: bank credentials, AI configuration, budgets, categories.

setup.workspaceTitle: What should we call this corner of your finances?
setup.workspaceDescriptionBefore: A workspace keeps one slice of your life isolated, with its own banks, categories, and budgets. Most people start with
setup.workspaceRenameHint: You can rename it later from Settings.
setup.bankDescription: Add every bank and card you want to track. Credentials are encrypted with AES-256 and stored on this machine only, never leaving your computer.
setup.bankSignInDescriptionSuffix: . They're encrypted locally, nothing leaves this machine.
setup.bankOneReady: One account ready. Add another or move on.
setup.bankManyReady: {count} accounts ready. Add another or move on.
setup.bankReadyDescription: You can always come back from Settings to add or remove accounts.
setup.bankConnectionWorks: ✓ Connection works. Click save to finish.
setup.bankAesNote: AES-256-GCM · stored on this machine only. Never sent to a server.
setup.twoFaProgrammaticHint: {bank} sends a one-time SMS code on first sync. Budgeteer will save a long-term token so future syncs don't need a fresh code.
setup.twoFaProgrammaticHintHasToken: You already have a saved token.
setup.twoFaManualHint: If you have 2FA enabled on {bank}, turn this on so Budgeteer opens a browser window when syncing this account. Solve the challenge in the popup and the sync will continue.
setup.twoFaProgrammaticNote: Not needed for {bank}. 2FA is handled programmatically.
setup.twoFaSavedTokenHint: Removing it forces a fresh SMS code on the next sync.
setup.aiDescription: Budgeteer uses AI to group your transactions into categories. You can change this any time in settings.
setup.aiClaudeEncryptedNote: Encrypted with AES-256-GCM and stored locally.
setup.aiManualNoteAfter: ; you can assign categories from the transactions table any time. Switch to Claude or Ollama later in
setup.targetDescription: A single number for how much you want to spend each month.
setup.targetInfoAfter: : green when you're tracking under, amber as you near the limit, red if you go over.
setup.targetChangeHint: You can change this any time in settings.
setup.budgetsTitle: Set a budget for each category
setup.budgetsDescription: Type an amount for the ones you want to cap. Leave blank to just track.
setup.budgetsSkip: Skip, auto-set from spending after first sync
setup.completeDescription: When you click below, Budgeteer will pull your transactions and bucket them up. You can re-sync any time from the dashboard or settings.
setup.completeImportFetch: Ready to fetch 90 days of activity
setup.aiProviderGeminiTagline: Google AI Studio, generous free tier
setup.aiManualNoteAfterWithGemini: ; you can assign categories from the transactions table any time. Switch to Claude, Gemini, or Ollama later in
setup.welcomeHeadline: Know where your month is heading
setup.welcomeSubhead: Connect your accounts once. Budgeteer forecasts your month, shows what's safe to spend, and finds ways to save, all on your own machine.
setup.welcomeValueAutoBody: Your transactions sync on their own, no spreadsheets.
setup.welcomeValueForecastBody: See if you'll finish the month in the plus, before it happens.
setup.welcomeValuePrivateBody: Open source and local-only. No cloud, no subscription.

id.label: ID Number
id.hint: Your 9-digit Israeli national ID (Teudat Zehut). Not your card number.

card6Digits.label: Last 6 Digits of Your Card
card6Digits.hint: The last 6 digits of your Isracard credit card number. This is NOT your ID.

isracardPassword.label: Isracard Password
isracardPassword.hint: The same password you use to log in on the Isracard website.

email.label: Email
email.hint: The email you use to sign in to One Zero.

phoneNumber.label: Phone number
phoneNumber.hint: Where the SMS one-time code will be sent. International format including the country code.

ollamaModels.llama3_2_3b: Recommended. Fast and accurate enough for categorizing.
ollamaModels.llama3_2_1b: Smallest and fastest. Slightly less accurate.

budgetDetail.addTransactionUnavailable: Manual transaction entry not available yet
budgetDetail.noTransactionsInPeriod: No transactions in this category for this period.

budgetDetail.childrenBannerRollup: Budget rolled up from sub-categories. Set a budget on this parent to override.

ollamaStatus.notInstalledBody: Ollama is a free, local AI that runs on your machine. It takes about a minute to install.
ollamaStatus.step1: Download and run the installer from ollama.com.
ollamaStatus.step2: Launch Ollama (it runs in the menu bar or system tray).
ollamaStatus.step3: Come back here. Budgeteer auto-starts Ollama and lets you download the model.
ollamaStatus.notReachable: Couldn't reach Ollama at this URL. If you've already installed it, double-check the URL above.
ollamaStatus.downloadPrompt: Download {model} now. You only need to do this once.
ollamaStatus.downloadPromptSized: Download {model} now (~{size} GB, a few minutes depending on your connection). You only need to do this once.

forecast.daysLeft: {days, plural, =0 {Last day of the month} one {# day left} other {# days left}}
forecast.verdictPlusTitle: On track to finish {month} in the plus
forecast.verdictPlusSub: About {amount} left over if this keeps up.
forecast.verdictTightTitle: {month} is going to be a close one
forecast.verdictTightSub: You're set to land right around even. A little care keeps you positive.
forecast.verdictMinusTitle: Heading for a tight spot this {month}
forecast.verdictMinusSub: About {amount} short if nothing changes. Small tweaks can turn it around.
forecast.addBalanceHint: Add your current balance to see where you'll land at month end.
forecast.emptyTitle: Your forecast appears once you sync
forecast.emptyBody: Connect a bank to pull in your transactions automatically, and we'll show how your month is shaping up.

recommendations.rec_overdraft-risk_title: Keep an eye on your balance
recommendations.rec_overdraft-risk_body: You're on track to end the month around {amount}. A few small trims keep you above zero.
recommendations.rec_minus-month_body: At this pace you'd finish about {amount} short. Around {amount2} a day turns it around.
recommendations.rec_tight-month_title: It's going to be a close month
recommendations.rec_tight-month_body: You're projected to land near even. Keeping to about {amount2} a day keeps you positive.
recommendations.rec_plus-month_body: On track to end about {amount} ahead. Lovely work.
recommendations.rec_safe-to-spend_body: About {amount} a day keeps you on track, roughly {amount2} this week.
recommendations.rec_cut-subscription_body: You pay about {amount} a month for {merchant}. Still getting your money's worth?
recommendations.rec_category-spike_body: About {amount} more than usual this month. Worth a quick look.
recommendations.rec_trim-category_body: Trimming {category} a little could free up about {amount} a month.
recommendations.rec_fees_body: About {amount} in fees lately. Many can be waived with a quick call.
recommendations.rec_build-savings_body: You're set to have about {amount} spare. Move some aside before it drifts.
recommendations.rec_add-balance_body: Enter today's balance to see exactly where you'll land at month end.

insights.savingsEmptyBody: Nice and lean. We'll flag opportunities as they appear.
insights.savSubscriptionBody: Recurring charge. Cancel if you're not using it.
insights.savSpikeBody: Higher than your usual this month.
insights.fixedSubtitle: Committed spend vs the flexible part
insights.recurringEmpty: No recurring charges detected yet. They appear once you have a few months of history.
insights.emptyBody: Connect a bank to pull in your transactions and unlock savings tips and your fixed-vs-variable split.

review.countMeta: {count, plural, one {# to review} other {# to review}}
review.emptyBody: Nothing needs review right now. Newly flagged transactions show up here after a sync.
review.groupFlaggedDescription: The categorizer guessed but wasn't sure. Confirm or fix each one.
review.groupUncategorizedDescription: No category yet, so these aren't in your breakdown.
review.groupTransferDescription: These look like moves between your own accounts, not real spending.
review.focusDoneBody: You've stepped through every flagged transaction.

anomalies.title: Worth a look
anomalies.subtitle: {count} to check this month
anomalies.emptyBody: No duplicate, foreign, or out-of-pattern charges this month.
anomalies.anom_duplicate-charge_body: Two charges of {amount} within a few days. Check it is not a mistake.
anomalies.anom_foreign-charge_body: {amount} from a merchant you have not used before.
anomalies.anom_new-subscription_body: Looks like a new recurring charge of {amount}/mo. Keep it?

home.title: Home
home.intro: What each card on your home page shows, and how the numbers are worked out.

cashFlow.title: Cash flow
cashFlow.body: Your income minus expenses for the selected period and accounts. Internal transfers between your own accounts, and credit-card bills that were matched to their individual purchases, are left out so the same money is never counted twice.
cashFlow.example: If ₪8,000 of salary arrives and ₪5,000 goes out, cash flow is +₪3,000. A ₪2,000 move from checking to savings does not change it, because the money never left you.

trend.title: Trend
trend.body: Income versus expenses month by month. Each transaction is counted in its Israel (Asia/Jerusalem) calendar month, based on the date it happened, so a late end-of-month purchase lands in the correct month.
trend.example: A purchase at 23:30 on May 31 (Israel time) counts in May, even if the bank's server stamped it just after midnight in another time zone.

typicalMonth.title: Typical month
typicalMonth.body: A baseline built from your recent completed months, used to tell whether the current month is running high or low.
typicalMonth.example: If the last three months were ₪9,000, ₪11,000 and ₪10,000, your typical month is ₪10,000, and spending ₪11,500 so far would read as above typical.

recentActivity.title: Recent activity
recentActivity.body: The latest transactions across the accounts you have selected, newest first. Internal transfers are hidden so the list reflects real activity.

flagged.title: Needs review
flagged.body: A count of transactions Budgeteer could not categorize with confidence. Clearing them keeps your totals and insights accurate.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.intro: How to read, filter, and interpret your transactions list.

kindFilter.title: Income / expense filter
kindFilter.body: Switch between all transactions, income only, or expenses only. Internal transfers are never shown as income or expense, so they stay out of both views.

accountFilter.title: Account filter
accountFilter.body: The account selector at the top of the app. Totals, rows, and every summary reflect only the accounts you pick, so you can isolate a single card or bank.

period.title: Month navigation
period.body: Move between months. Rows are grouped by their Israel (Asia/Jerusalem) calendar date, which is the date the transaction happened.
period.example: A credit-card purchase made on May 3 appears under May here, even though the card company will bill it to your bank in June.

cardMatch.title: Card-bill match
cardMatch.body: When a bank charge is a credit-card bill, a badge links it to the individual card purchases that make it up. The bill is treated as a transfer and kept out of expense totals, so you are not counted twice: once for the purchases and again for the bill that pays them. A bill we could not match (for example a card you have not connected) is still counted as a single expense, so that spending is never lost.
cardMatch.example: A ₪1,896 Visa bill is paid from checking. If those purchases were already pulled from the card, the bill is hidden and the purchases are counted instead. If the card is not connected, the ₪1,896 bill is counted on its own.

rowStates.title: Row states
rowStates.body: 'Needs review' marks an uncertain category waiting for you; 'excluded' rows are deliberately kept out of every total; a card-match badge marks a bill linked to its purchases.

review.title: Review
review.intro: Clear the queue of transactions that need your attention.

whyFlagged.title: Why it's flagged
whyFlagged.body: Either the AI was not confident about the category it chose, or it is an expense with no category yet. Both keep an item in the queue until you confirm it.

queue.title: The queue
queue.body: Items are ordered so the ones most needing review come first, and they leave the list the moment you categorize or confirm them.

actions.title: Actions
actions.body: Assign a category to clear an item, or mark it reviewed as-is when the existing guess is correct.

cardMatch.title: Card-bill match
cardMatch.body: A badge links a credit-card bill to the card purchases behind it, so the bill is treated as a transfer and not counted as extra spending.
cardMatch.example: A ₪1,896 card bill matched to its purchases is excluded from expenses; the underlying purchases are what count toward your spending.

budget.title: Budget
budget.intro: How budgets are set and tracked.

autoVsManual.title: Auto vs manual
autoVsManual.body: Auto budgets follow a rolling average of your own spending and adjust over time; manual budgets are a fixed monthly amount you set yourself.

average.title: How the average works
average.body: An auto budget is the average expense for that category over the last three completed months. The current, in-progress month is not used, so a partial month does not drag the number down.
average.example: If groceries were ₪1,800, ₪2,100 and ₪2,100 over the last three full months, the auto budget is ₪2,000.

spendBars.title: Spend vs budget
spendBars.body: Each bar shows how much you have spent this month against the budgeted amount, so you can see at a glance where you are over or under.

detail.title: Category detail
detail.body: Open a category to see the individual transactions behind its total and understand what is driving the spend.

insights.title: Insights
insights.intro: Patterns and projections drawn from your spending.

anomalies.title: Anomalies
anomalies.body: Charges that stand out from your normal pattern for a category, flagged when they are clearly larger than usual rather than for small fluctuations.
anomalies.example: If pharmacies are usually about ₪300 a month and this month they reach ₪900, that threefold jump is flagged as an anomaly.

recommendations.title: Recommendations
recommendations.body: Suggested actions based on how you actually spend, such as a category that has been creeping up over several months.

forecast.title: Forecast
forecast.body: A projection of your total spending by the end of the month. It takes what you have spent so far and scales it across the whole month at your current daily pace.
forecast.example: By the 10th of a 30-day month you have spent ₪4,000. At that pace the forecast is ₪4,000 ÷ 10 × 30 = ₪12,000.

ranges.title: Time ranges
ranges.body: Monthly and month-to-date windows, anchored to the Israel (Asia/Jerusalem) calendar so comparisons line up day for day.
